title: Till I Wake
content:
When I am dying, lean over me tenderly, softly,
        Stoop, as the yellow roses droop in the wind from the South.
    So I may, when I wake, if there be an Awakening,
        Keep, what lulled me to sleep, the touch of your lips on my mouth.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Laurence Hope (Adela Florence Cory Nicolson), titled "Till I Wake", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
